caveats: a: The occupation source is secondary and reports employment rather than payroll weights.
a: Published occupation shares are for NAICS 42448 and include firms of all sizes.
a: Exposure is to tasks not yet automated, which the source does not measure directly.
rho: No industry-wide five-year implementation study was found.
rho: Implementation varies sharply with ERP quality, SKU complexity, and customer integration.
e: The supplied firm count is itself an estimate and may include borderline business models.
e: The ERS channel data are older and cover grocery wholesaling broadly rather than the LMM band alone.
s5: The Gallup sample spans all industries and business sizes.
s5: Plans are not completed transfers, and gifts or public offerings may not meet the qualifying-control definition.
q: No direct study measures AI-benefit pass-through in produce distribution.
q: Retention will be lower for spot-like commodity accounts and higher for differentiated service bundles.
d5: Fresh-fruit and fresh-vegetable trends differ by commodity.
d5: Availability is a consumption proxy and does not isolate the merchant-wholesale channel.
d5: Constant-quality service demand can diverge from food tonnage as value-added handling changes.
o: The value is the operator-required share of year-five quantity, not the independent-wholesaler market share.
o: Large customers can internalize procurement and logistics even when the functions remain necessary.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring B2B product resale with a gross merchant margin, often augmented by cold-chain handling, repacking, delivery, category knowledge, and availability assurance; customer orders and commodity prices can reset frequently.
lens.excluded_activities: Manufacturer-owned sales branches, brokers that do not own or handle product, captive internal distribution units, shell entities, non-control financing vehicles, and firms outside the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Independent merchant wholesalers that repeatedly procure, sell, and often receive, store, repack, ripen, or deliver fresh fruits and vegetables for external retail, foodservice, institutional, and wholesale customers.

a.bridge: The source measures headcount shares, not wage-weighted task exposure: 20% sales, 22% office/administrative support, 7% management, 6% production, and 30% transportation/material moving in NAICS 42448. I apply higher task exposure to quoting, ordering, customer service, scheduling, documentation, and management analysis, and low exposure to driving and physical handling. Wage weighting likely raises the result relative to a simple headcount calculation because management and sales are better paid; the absence of task-level and wage microdata leaves two-sided error.
caveats: The occupation source is secondary and reports employment rather than payroll weights.
Published occupation shares are for NAICS 42448 and include firms of all sizes.
Exposure is to tasks not yet automated, which the source does not measure directly.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted occupation file for sampled 424480 firms mapped to O*NET tasks, plus time-and-motion logs separating transactional sales/admin work from relationship selling and exception handling.
a.rationale: The largest implementable opportunity is in order intake, quote preparation, account correspondence, invoice and PACA-document checks, demand forecasting, purchasing support, and dispatch planning. Physical inspection, spoilage decisions, warehouse movement, and driving remain much less substitutable, limiting the whole-workforce share.

d5.bridge: USDA food-availability data proxy physical produce demand rather than wholesaler service-basket quantity. Fresh vegetable availability rose from 142 pounds per person in 1996 to 148 in 2024 even as total vegetables weakened, while imports supplied 59% of fresh-fruit and 35% of fresh-vegetable availability in 2023. I combine roughly stable per-capita fresh demand with U.S. population growth and allow direct sourcing or channel-share shifts to create downside; availability includes loss and is not actual consumption.
caveats: Fresh-fruit and fresh-vegetable trends differ by commodity.
Availability is a consumption proxy and does not isolate the merchant-wholesale channel.
Constant-quality service demand can diverge from food tonnage as value-added handling changes.
d5.change_evidence: USDA five-year forecasts for fresh fruit and vegetable domestic disappearance by commodity, paired with Census channel sales and customer procurement-share data separating merchant-wholesaler volume from direct grower-to-retailer flows.
d5.rationale: The physical fresh-produce basket is mature but essential. Modest population growth and year-round imported assortment support slight volume growth, while affordability, changing diets, waste reduction, retailer consolidation, and direct procurement create downside.

caveats: The supplied firm count is itself an estimate and may include borderline business models.
The ERS channel data are older and cover grocery wholesaling broadly rather than the LMM band alone.
e.change_evidence: A stratified review of 424480 firms in commercial databases, with ownership, customer concentration, related-party sales, physical handling, recurring-customer revenue, and normalized EBITDA verified from confidential financials.
e.rationale: Most merchant wholesalers in the supplied LMM count sell repeatedly to external businesses and fit the lens. Ineligible cases include quasi-captive operations, entities economically dependent on one grower or customer, businesses without a transferable operating organization, and firms whose economics are principally brokerage despite their classification.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 424480 ($6,660,557,398) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 424480 ($116,923,779,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1137 (margins.json[42]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Retail (Distributors) EBITDA/Sales 11.37%); revenue/employee $1,101,268 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 424480); class <5: 2,238 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,061,526 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $348,095 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 757 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,693,930 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,215,900 -> IN $1-10M band; class 10-19: 536 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,128,358 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,061,194 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 702 firms, avg revenue/firm $44,526,828 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,062,700 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 196 firms, avg revenue/firm $152,308,495 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,317,476 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 51 firms, avg revenue/firm $610,778,059 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $69,445,465 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,995

caveats: The value is the operator-required share of year-five quantity, not the independent-wholesaler market share.
Large customers can internalize procurement and logistics even when the functions remain necessary.
o.change_evidence: Longitudinal Census of Wholesale Trade channel shares, major-retailer supplier filings, and interviews with growers and buyers quantifying volume moved direct, through integrated distribution, through brokers, and through independent merchant wholesalers.
o.rationale: Even with better software and more direct trading, fresh produce still requires accountable procurement, quality acceptance, food-safety and payment compliance, assortment decisions, exception management, and often cold-chain handling and delivery. Some quantity can bypass independent wholesalers through direct retailer sourcing, integrated distribution, brokers, or digital marketplaces, but software alone does not perform the physical and legal operator role.

caveats: No direct study measures AI-benefit pass-through in produce distribution.
Retention will be lower for spot-like commodity accounts and higher for differentiated service bundles.
q.change_evidence: Customer-contract samples and invoice-level panel data linking realized gross margin to cost-to-serve reductions across renewals, including win/loss behavior, rebates, shrink, and customer concentration.
q.rationale: AI savings initially accrue to the operator, but recurring B2B orders, transparent commodity markets, buyer consolidation, frequent repricing, and competing distributors should return much of the benefit to customers. Specialized service, reliability, cold-chain execution, and customer-specific assortment allow partial retention; the estimate refers only to implemented gross benefit and does not subtract implementation difficulty again.

caveats: No industry-wide five-year implementation study was found.
Implementation varies sharply with ERP quality, SKU complexity, and customer integration.
rho.change_evidence: Pre/post deployment studies from produce distributors showing task-level hours, exception rates, forecast error, shrink, customer complaints, and headcount avoided over at least twelve months.
rho.rationale: Within exposed work, mainstream tools should automate routine order capture, document extraction, pricing recommendations, replenishment forecasts, and customer communications within five years. Adoption is held back by fragmented systems, thin data, freshness and substitution exceptions, rapid commodity changes, customer-specific terms, and the need for accountable review of PACA-sensitive transactions.

s5.bridge: Gallup measured plans among U.S. business owners across industries in fall 2024: 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership within five years. I use that employer-firm rate as the center, reduce it slightly for failures to execute and non-control gifts or reorganizations, and allow upward error because established produce wholesalers may have older owners and transferable assets. The proxy is not specific to NAICS 424480, EBITDA size, or completed transactions.
caveats: The Gallup sample spans all industries and business sizes.
Plans are not completed transfers, and gifts or public offerings may not meet the qualifying-control definition.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of closed control transactions for 424480 from PitchBook, Capital IQ, Axial, BizBuySell, and produce-industry advisers, deduplicated and divided by an eligible-firm cohort; owner-age tabulations by detailed NAICS would refine the prior.
s5.rationale: A meaningful minority of eligible firms should transfer control, but expressed intent overstates completed qualifying deals and some owners will close, retain family control, or defer. The base therefore sits below the observed employer-owner plan rate.

principal_driver: Repeat, exception-heavy ordering and coordination work sits beside an enduring physical cold-chain service requirement.
principal_weakness: Transparent commodity pricing and powerful buyers can rapidly compete away operator savings.

run_meta.title: Fresh Fruit and Vegetable Merchant Wholesalers

sources.supports: Documents produce sellers' statutory trust, transaction records, typical 10-day prompt payment, written alternative terms, and 30-day maximum terms for trust protection.

sources.title: Vegetable availability declined in 2024
